task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly calculates the median of a list, handling edge cases such as an empty list.
trace: To solve the task, I first sorted the input list 'nums' and checked if it was empty. If empty, I returned None as requested. Then, I calculated the index of the middle element in the sorted list by dividing the length by 2 (rounded down for odd lengths). After that, I handled both cases: if the length is odd, I simply returned the middle element. If the length is even, I averaged the two middle elements and returned the result as a float. To handle an edge case, I took care of the situation where the list has only one element (length 1), which would be considered the median in that case.
